name: 64x160 main + 64x96 tail widened fixed split

summary.name: 64x160 main + 64x96 tail widened fixed split
summary.hypothesis: Round 15 proved that trimming per-warp N baggage can reduce MIO throttle, but the 32x128 retile cratered because it doubled down on CTA count and drove DRAM to 78.82% with a register occupancy cap of 6. Keep the round-14 vectorized epilogue and widen the fixed-shape main kernel to 64x160 across the first 7680 columns so the hot region drops from 60 CTAs to 48 CTAs per row while preserving the proven 64x96 tail.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Main-path operand movement and memory traffic from the too-narrow round-15 split; a 64x160 steady-state should improve reuse and reduce launch pressure without reopening generic tail overhead.
code_locations: src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:28-79 add a 64x160 TileConfig alias and retune the fixed-shape main/tail constants for a 7680-column main region plus a 96-column tail
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:132-145 and 190-257 update B-stage copy counts, shared-stride math, and the tensor-core K-loop for the wider main-path specialization
src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:265-287 and 320-339 keep the round-14 vectorized epilogue behavior, but launch a 48x101 64x160 main grid ahead of the existing 64x96 tail
summary.risk: Moderate. The wider B tile raises shared footprint and live fragment count, but it is the cleanest widened-main follow-up because it reduces CTA count without introducing an extra middle cleanup stage.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if the wider main tile still leaves DRAM near the round-15 level, or if register pressure materially worsens occupancy without a clear runtime win, because then 64x160 is not wide enough to justify the extra tile state.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
